ACT_NOW.author: trq212
ACT_NOW.one_liner: A Claude coding tip promises to improve developer efficiency by demonstrating a specific action or insight but needs link or media analysis for details.
ACT_NOW.reason: We haven't explicitly focused on optimizing prompt caching in Claude Code, despite using it extensively. The detailed blog post offers actionable strategies that can improve efficiency.
relevance: Directly relevant to Claude Code's performance.
Relates to efficiency in our use of Claude for code generation and manipulation.
Maintaining static system prompts and toolsets, while dynamically managing session context, optimizes caching, so we need to evaluate our session management.
Monitoring cache hit rates is essential for identifying and addressing cache-breaking incidents, so we need to track our cache performance, which we haven't done before.
ACT_NOW.proposed_action: Read the full article and discuss strategies for optimizing prompt caching in our Claude Code workflow. Add cache hit rate monitoring.

ACT_NOW.author: repligate
ACT_NOW.text_preview:
PSA: Claude Code automatically DELETES sessions that have been inactive for more than 30 days.

Disable this by setting
ACT_NOW.one_liner: Prevent automatic deletion of Claude Code sessions inactive for 30 days by setting `cleanupPeriodDays` to a large number in `~/.claude/settings.json`.
ACT_NOW.reason: We actively use Claude Code Desktop and this default behavior of automatic session deletion is new information. This prevents unintentional loss of work and requires immediate action.
relevance: Claude Code Desktop is our primary interface.
ACT_NOW.proposed_action: Implement the suggested change in ~/.claude/settings.json on all relevant systems to prevent session loss.

EXPERIMENT.author: trq212
EXPERIMENT.text_preview:
We've rolled out a new auto-memory feature.

Claude now remembers what it learns across sessions — your project context,
EXPERIMENT.one_liner: Claude's new auto-memory feature allows it to learn and remember information across sessions for improved assistance.
EXPERIMENT.reason: While we use Claude for planning, this "auto-memory" feature significantly changes how we interact with the model. It's worth experimenting with to see if it improves efficiency in planning and handoff document generation.
EXPERIMENT.proposed_action: Test the auto-memory feature in a planning session for the bookmark monitor and compare the handoff document quality to previous methods.

EXPERIMENT.author: IamEmily2050
EXPERIMENT.text_preview: I just published my first skill on Github, this is for Seedance V2, hopefully it will help so many people to write good
EXPERIMENT.one_liner: New Seedance V2 skill released on Github aims to improve video prompt engineering workflows by providing example prompts.
EXPERIMENT.reason: The tweet describes a new skill published on Github to help users with prompt engineering and video creation. While we already use skills, this specific collection of skills for video generation is new and could potentially inform or improve our own video-related tasks in the hall-of-fake project.
EXPERIMENT.proposed_action: Explore the skill repository on Github to assess its relevance and potential applicability to our hall-of-fake project.

EXPERIMENT.author: ericzakariasson
EXPERIMENT.one_liner: This Claude code tip promises to enhance coding workflows within the Claude environment.
EXPERIMENT.reason: The tweet describes a method for improving coding agent memory by using dynamic context discovery of chat transcripts. This addresses a key challenge in building effective coding agents, and while we aren't explicitly focused on long-running coding agents currently, the underlying technique of persistent context and learning from past interactions is directly relevant to our experiments with cross-model review and long-running task recovery (Ralph Wiggum).
EXPERIMENT.proposed_action: Evaluate the feasibility of integrating a similar dynamic context discovery mechanism into our cross-model review process or long-running task recovery strategy.

EXPERIMENT.author: VictorTaelin
EXPERIMENT.text_preview:
continual learning, only continual learning, and nothing other than continual learning, is what's missing right now

I c
EXPERIMENT.one_liner: AI development should prioritize continual learning over benchmark performance to build models that retain knowledge and improve productivity across sessions.
EXPERIMENT.reason: We're actively building autonomous agents and addressing long-context issues, and continual learning is a core challenge for these systems. This highlights the importance of addressing information retention and contextual overload.
relevance: Autonomous daily bookmark monitor (analysis engine in progress)
EXPERIMENT.proposed_action: Research continual learning techniques and their applicability to our agent architecture. Consider how to evaluate these techniques in the context of our bookmark monitor.

EXPERIMENT.author: amorriscode
EXPERIMENT.text_preview: y’all want this in the Code tab? 👀
EXPERIMENT.one_liner: Claude hints at a new Code tab feature, prompting user feedback on a possible tool integration.
EXPERIMENT.reason: A new feature in the Claude Code tab could streamline our coding workflow, particularly given our reliance on Claude Code Desktop. It warrants experimentation to see if it enhances our existing processes.
relevance: Delegation pattern (Claude.ai for planning, Claude Code for execution)
EXPERIMENT.proposed_action: Monitor for further details and test the feature when it becomes available.

EXPERIMENT.author: simonw
EXPERIMENT.text_preview: Brief notes on Claude Code Remote and Cowork scheduled tasks - both of which overlap with OpenClaw, and both of which re
EXPERIMENT.one_liner: Learn about Claude Code's Remote and Cowork task scheduling features, similar to OpenClaw, which require a powered-on remote machine for code execution.
EXPERIMENT.reason: While we've decided to skip Cowork, the "remote control" feature of Claude Code *itself* (not Cowork) warrants exploration. It's a new execution modality for Claude Code that might be useful.
relevance: Leverages Claude Code which we use daily.
Offers remote code execution which aligns with our needs.
Addresses limitations of Cowork by potentially being more integrated.
EXPERIMENT.proposed_action: Briefly test Claude Code's remote control feature to assess its stability and potential integration with our workflows.

EXPERIMENT.author: scaling01
EXPERIMENT.one_liner: This Claude tip probably showcases a code optimization or workflow enhancement technique using specific commands/tools that can be found by clicking the link.

Introducing: built-in git worktree support for Claude Code 

Now, agents can run in parallel without interfering with on

relevance: Update on feature of existing tool (Claude Code).

NOTED.author: Econ_Marshall
